channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: White House Seeks to Slash CISA Funding by $707 Million
item.description: The Trump administration says the FY2027 budget refocuses CISA on its core mission: protecting federal agencies and critical infrastructure. The post White House Seeks to Slash CISA Funding by $707 Million appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Increased Durable Object Errors in AMS
item.description: Apr 7, 07:31 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Apr 7, 07:17 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is aware of, and investigating an issue with Durable Objects which potentially impacts multiple customers in the AMS region. Durable Objects are experiencing an...

item.title: ModelBest Raises Funding, Enters USD 1 Billion+ Foundation Model Unicorn Tier
item.description: Chinese on‑device AI foundation model maker ModelBest raised several hundred million RMB (≈ USD tens of millions), led by Shenzhen Capital Group and Inovance Capital, reaching unicorn status after three rounds in the past year.

item.title: Xiaomi to Launch REDMI K90 Max With Active Cooling
item.description: Xiaomi announced the REDMI K90 Max, launching this month as the first K‑series Max model, featuring an active air‑cooling fan, 165Hz display, and dual‑chip architecture.

item.title: Xiaomi Unveils YU7 Maison Margiela Edition Concept Vehicle
item.description: Xiaomi unveiled a special‑edition YU7 with French fashion house Maison Margiela, featuring a minimalist white exterior and brand elements, but the vehicle is a one‑off showpiece, not for sale.

item.title: Huawei-Backed AITO M9 2026 Model Spotted Ahead of Launch
item.description: The 2026 AITO M9 has been spotted on Chinese roads without camouflage, featuring redesigned door handles, a more angular profile, and Huawei’s next‑gen image‑grade LiDAR.

item.title: Spirit AI Raises USD 420 Million in 30 Days, Backed by Lei Jun and Jack Ma Funds
item.description: Embodied AI startup Spirit AI raised RMB 3 billion (≈ USD 420 million) in two rounds over 30 days, backed by Shunwei Capital and Yunfeng Fund (affiliated with Lei Jun and Jack Ma).

item.title: China-Linked Storm-1175 Exploits Zero-Days to Rapidly Deploy Medusa Ransomware
item.description: A China-based threat actor known for deploying Medusa ransomware has been linked to the weaponization of a combination of zero-day and N-day vulnerabilities to orchestrate "high-velocity" attacks and break into susceptible internet-facing systems. "The threat actor's high operational...

item.title: Wynn Resorts Says 21,000 Employees Affected by ShinyHunters Hack
item.description: The high-end casino and hotel operator has likely paid a ransom to avoid a data leak. The post Wynn Resorts Says 21,000 Employees Affected by ShinyHunters Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Flowise AI Agent Builder Under Active CVSS 10.0 RCE Exploitation; 12,000+ Instances Exposed
item.description: Threat actors are exploiting a maximum-severity security flaw in Flowise, an open-source artificial intelligence (AI) platform, according to new findings from VulnCheck. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2025-59528 (CVSS score: 10.0), a code injection vulnerability that could result in...

item.title: IC3-Evolve: Proof-/Witness-Gated Offline LLM-Driven Heuristic Evolution for IC3 Hardware Model Checking
item.description: arXiv:2604.03232v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: IC3, also known as property-directed reachability (PDR), is a commonly-used algorithm for hardware safety model checking. It checks if a state transition system complies with a given safety property. IC3 either returns UNSAFE (indicating property...

item.title: Structural Segmentation of the Minimum Set Cover Problem: Exploiting Universe Decomposability for Metaheuristic Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2604.03234v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Minimum Set Cover Problem (MSCP) is a classical NP-hard combinatorial optimization problem with numerous applications in science and engineering. Although a wide range of exact, approximate, and metaheuristic approaches have been proposed, most...

item.title: To Throw a Stone with Six Birds: On Agents and Agenthood
item.description: arXiv:2604.03239v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Six Birds Theory (SBT) treats macroscopic objects as induced closures rather than primitives. Empirical discussions of agency often conflate persistence (being an object) with control (making a counterfactual difference), which makes agency claims...

item.title: Position: Science of AI Evaluation Requires Item-level Benchmark Data
item.description: arXiv:2604.03244v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI evaluations have become the primary evidence for deploying generative AI systems across high-stakes domains. However, current evaluation paradigms often exhibit systemic validity failures. These issues, ranging from unjustified design choices to...

item.title: Toward Full Autonomous Laboratory Instrumentation Control with Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.03286v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The control of complex laboratory instrumentation often requires significant programming expertise, creating a barrier for researchers lacking computational skills. This work explores the potential of large language models (LLMs), such as ChatGPT, and...

item.title: Integrating Artificial Intelligence, Physics, and Internet of Things: A Framework for Cultural Heritage Conservation
item.description: arXiv:2604.03233v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The conservation of cultural heritage increasingly relies on integrating technological innovation with domain expertise to ensure effective monitoring and predictive maintenance. This paper presents a novel framework to support the preservation of...

item.title: Scaling DPPs for RAG: Density Meets Diversity
item.description: arXiv:2604.03240v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) enhances Large Language Models (LLMs) by grounding generation in external knowledge, yielding relevance responses that are aligned with factual evidence and evolving corpora. Standard RAG pipelines construct...

item.title: DRAFT: Task Decoupled Latent Reasoning for Agent Safety
item.description: arXiv:2604.03242v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The advent of tool-using LLM agents shifts safety monitoring from output moderation to auditing long, noisy interaction trajectories, where risk-critical evidence is sparse-making standard binary supervision poorly suited for credit assignment. To...

item.title: General Explicit Network (GEN): A novel deep learning architecture for solving partial differential equations
item.description: arXiv:2604.03321v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Machine learning, especially physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) and their neural network variants, has been widely used to solve problems involving partial differential equations (PDEs). The successful deployment of such methods beyond academic...

item.title: Apparent Age Estimation: Challenges and Outcomes
item.description: arXiv:2604.03335v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Apparent age estimation is a valuable tool for business personalization, yet current models frequently exhibit demographic biases. We review prior works on the DEX method by applying distribution learning techniques such as Mean-Variance Loss (MVL)...

item.title: Self-Execution Simulation Improves Coding Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.03253v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A promising research direction in enabling LLMs to generate consistently correct code involves addressing their inability to properly estimate program execution, particularly for code they generate. In this work, we demonstrate that Code LLMs can be...

item.title: Robust LLM Performance Certification via Constrained Maximum Likelihood Estimation
item.description: arXiv:2604.03257v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The ability to rigorously estimate the failure rates of large language models (LLMs) is a prerequisite for their safe deployment. Currently, however, practitioners often face a tradeoff between expensive human gold standards and potentially...

item.title: SoLA: Leveraging Soft Activation Sparsity and Low-Rank Decomposition for Large Language Model Compression
item.description: arXiv:2604.03258v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated impressive capabilities across various tasks, but the billion-scale parameters pose deployment challenges. Although existing methods attempt to reduce the scale of LLMs, they require either special...

item.title: Why Attend to Everything? Focus is the Key
item.description: arXiv:2604.03260v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce Focus, a method that learns which token pairs matter rather than approximating all of them. Learnable centroids assign tokens to groups; distant attention is restricted to same-group pairs while local attention operates at full...

item.title: VIGIL: An Extensible System for Real-Time Detection and Mitigation of Cognitive Bias Triggers
item.description: arXiv:2604.03261v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rise of generative AI is posing increasing risks to online information integrity and civic discourse. Most concretely, such risks can materialise in the form of mis- and disinformation. As a mitigation, media-literacy and transparency tools have...

item.title: Smart Routing currently has reduced performance
item.description: Apr 7, 00:27 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to work on a fix for this issue.Apr 6, 23:56 UTCIdentified - Cloudflare has identified performance issues with Smart Routing. We have identified and applied related mitigations

item.title: [AINews] Gemma 4 crosses 2 million downloads
item.description: a quiet day lets us give due respect to the enormously successful Gemma 4 launch

item.title: Cloudflare API Availability
item.description: Apr 6, 22:42 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 6, 21:38 UTCMonitoring - Cloudflare resolved issues with Cloudflare Dashboard and related APIs. These issues do not affect the serving of cached files via the Cloudflare CDN or other security features at the Cloudflare Edge. Customers...

item.title: Sonnet 4.6 elevated rate of errors
item.description: Apr 6, 21:45 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 6, 21:26 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Iran-Linked Password-Spraying Campaign Targets 300+ Israeli Microsoft 365 Organizations
item.description: An Iran-nexus threat actor is suspected to be behind a password-spraying campaign targeting Microsoft 365 environments in Israel and the U.A.E. amid ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The activity, assessed to be ongoing, was carried out in three distinct attack waves that...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude.ai
item.description: Apr 6, 16:44 UTCMonitoring - We have applied a fix, and are seeing success rates recover across affected services. We are continuing to monitor to ensure there is no recurrence of issues.Apr 6, 16:17 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to work on a fix for this issue.Apr 6, 15:54 UTCUpdate - This issue...

item.title: The one piece of data that could actually shed light on your job and AI
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Within Silicon Valley’s orbit, an AI-fueled jobs apocalypse is spoken about as a given. The mood is so grim that a societal impacts researcher at Anthropic,...

item.title: DPRK-Linked Hackers Use GitHub as C2 in Multi-Stage Attacks Targeting South Korea
item.description: Threat actors likely associated with the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) have been observed using GitHub as command-and-control (C2) infrastructure in multi-stage attacks targeting organizations in South Korea. The attack chain, per Fortinet FortiGuard Labs,...

item.title: Google DeepMind Researchers Map Web Attacks Against AI Agents
item.description: A vulnerability named ‘AI Agent Traps’ allows attackers to manipulate, deceive, and exploit visiting agents via malicious web content. The post Google DeepMind Researchers Map Web Attacks Against AI Agents appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Digital experience Logpush delay
item.description: Apr 6, 15:18 UTCIdentified - Cloudflare One customers that use LogPush to ingest DEX Device State Events will see up to a 20 minute delay. DEX data in the dashboard and the API is not impacted

item.title: Delay in some zone configuration deployment
item.description: Apr 6, 15:00 UTCResolved - We have resolved an issue that caused a minor delay in configuration deployments across a small number of zones between 14:56 and 20:36 UTC on April 5, 2026.

item.title: Multi-OS Cyberattacks: How SOCs Close a Critical Risk in 3 Steps
item.description: Your attack surface no longer lives on one operating system, and neither do the campaigns targeting it. In enterprise environments, attackers move across Windows endpoints, executive MacBooks, Linux infrastructure, and mobile devices, taking advantage of the fact that many...

item.title: Guardarian Users Targeted With Malicious Strapi NPM Packages
item.description: Hackers published 36 NPM packages posing as Strapi plugins to execute shells, escape containers, and harvest credentials. The post Guardarian Users Targeted With Malicious Strapi NPM Packages appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: North Korean Hackers Target High-Profile Node.js Maintainers
item.description: The threat actor behind the Axios supply chain attack has been aiming at other maintainers in its social engineering campaign. The post North Korean Hackers Target High-Profile Node.js Maintainers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: AI is changing how small online sellers decide what to make
item.description: For years Mike McClary sold the Guardian LTE Flashlight, a heavy-duty black model, online through his small outdoor brand. The product, designed for brightness and durability, became one of his most popular items ever. Even after he stopped offering it around 2017, customers kept sending him emails...

item.title: GET /v1/responses endpoint is currently down and unable to serve requests
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Responses (Operational)

item.title: Some users may experience empty response from ChatGPT in web
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Conversations (Operational)

item.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
item.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer...

item.title: Sonnet 4.6 and Opus 4.6 elevated error rate
item.description: Apr 4, 17:30 UTCResolved - Between 10:09 and 10:31 PDT, Sonnet 4.6 and Opus 4.6 experienced elevated error rates across most product surfaces.

item.title: [AINews] Good Friday
item.description: a quiet day.

item.title: Elevated errors on requests to Claude Sonnet 4.6
item.description: Apr 3, 19:21 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 3, 18:12 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating an issue resulting in elevated errors on requests to Claude Sonnet 4.6. We will provide an update as soon as possible.

item.title: Four things we’d need to put data centers in space
item.description: MIT Technology Review Explains: Let our writers untangle the complex, messy world of technology to help you understand what’s coming next. You can read more from the series here. In January, Elon Musk’s SpaceX filed an application with the US Federal Communications Commission to launch up to one...

item.title: Marc Andreessen introspects on The Death of the Browser, Pi + OpenClaw, and Why "This Time Is Different"
item.description: The legend needs no intro... if you pardon our pun

item.title: Gemma 4 and what makes an open model succeed
item.description: Hint: it's not benchmark scores.

item.title: [AINews] Gemma 4: The best small Multimodal Open Models, dramatically better than Gemma 3 in every way
item.description: A welcome update from Google!
